div.p: Auto-checkpoint threshold = 1000 frames · Reader R1 snapshot = frame 500

g.text: ① Initial State — WAL Exceeds Threshold
② Auto-Checkpoint Triggers — Safe Limit Computed
③ Copy Frames 0–499 to Main Database
④ fsync Main Database — Durability Barrier
⑤ Partial WAL Reset — R1 Blocks Full Truncation
⑥ R1 Releases Snapshot → Full Checkpoint Completes
frames 0–499 | pages written BEFORE R1 snapshot
frames 500–1199 | pages written AFTER R1 snapshot
frame 0 → page 42 | pwrite(db_fd, buf, PAGE_SIZE, offset)
frame 1 → page 77 | pwrite(db_fd, buf, PAGE_SIZE, offset)
frame 2 → page 42 | OVERWRITES frame 0 — latest version wins
frame 499 → page 99 | pwrite(db_fd, buf, PAGE_SIZE, offset)
Only latest frame per page_no lands on disk

text.tspan: Flush OS page cache → physical medium

g.text: Pages from frames 0–499 | DURABLE on disk
Main DB consistent | YES — up to safe_limit 500
frames 0–499 | CHECKPOINTED — data now in main DB
frames 500–1199 | STILL IN WAL — R1 needs these
frames 1200+ | future writes continue appending here
frame_count | NOT reset to 0 — remains at 1200
7. Old frames invalidated by new salts
main DB durable up to frame 499
